title: isoetes riparia var. riparia
intro: Isoetes riparia var. riparia, also known as the riparian quillwort, is a rare and endemic aquatic plant species found in North America, characterized by its unique, quill-shaped leaves and rhizome. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which may be attributed to its high concentration of phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Isoetes riparia var. riparia are still being studied, but potential benefits may include reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in decoctions or infusions, although its availability and safety have not been extensively scientifically evaluated.
